plan_section: Step 2: Build and supervise Next + CoreScope in one Docker container

plan_alignment.notes: Implementation matches Step 2: one image builds Next and CoreScope binaries, supervises nginx/Next/CoreScope server/optional ingestor, keeps public port 3000, and persists CoreScope data.

correctness_safety.notes: nginx route ordering preserves Next /api/map/* and /api/live-map/* while routing CoreScope APIs, /map, /ws, and root WebSocket upgrades to CoreScope. Default ingestor-disabled startup is safe without MQTT credentials.

code_quality.notes: Dockerfile stages, supervisor config, startup script, and nginx routing are readable and consistent with the planned single-container architecture.

completeness.notes: Acceptance criteria and reported smoke checks pass. Minor non-blocking operational/asset polish noted below.

patterns.notes: Follows the planned supervisor plus nginx pattern and avoids editing upstream CoreScope source directly.

integration.notes: Ports, compose volume, package script, submodule checker, and CoreScope runtime paths integrate cleanly with the existing Next standalone container flow.

flags.note: CoreScope public/index.html references favicon.ico, but nginx explicitly routes favicon.svg only. Non-blocking because favicon.svg is available, but consider adding favicon.ico to the CoreScope asset route.

If CORESCOPE_ENABLE_INGESTOR=true is set before Step 3 config rendering/MQTT credentials exist, the ingestor may fail and be restarted by supervisor. Default false satisfies Step 2, but document or guard this before enabling ingestion.
